method: ran the proof as a subprocess (unsealed (isolation='none')); verdict = exit code (0 → green, else red)

evidence.stdout_tail:
PASS  test_the_report_gathers_one_pointers_transaction_ordered_and_isolated
  PASS  test_the_interpreter_reads_it_does_not_mutate
  PASS  test_a_first_miss_is_learned_and_folded_forward
  PASS  test_a_learned_gap_that_recurs_is_loud
  PASS  test_a_complete_report_is_clean

  PASS  test_learned_completeness_persists
green — diagnostic_interpreter: assembles one pointer's transaction into an ordered, isolated first-pass report, and the learning-loop folds a first miss forward (Law 1) while surfacing an already-learned recurrence LOUD (Law 7) — the special-class falsifier.

seal.detail: none: no seal requested — the route is open by construction, and the record says so

falsifier: the same proof exits non-zero on re-run, or the code it proves changes underneath it
horizon: valid until the proof file or the code it proves changes (Law 3: a VALIDATION expires)
